content.text:
{
  "dimension": "intervention-quality",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach makes a series of well-matched, well-timed moves that follow the AoA coaching path without dismissing the client's framework. Turn 2 catches the somatic tell (the smile) and uses it as the entry point rather than engaging with IFS conceptually. Turn 4 stays with the exhaustion rather than rushing forward — a clear regulation/feeling-before-story move. Turn 6 reframes the 'terrible admission' and asks the precise question that loosens the framework's grip without attacking it. Turn 8 is the pivotal move: instead of asking 'what's you?' prematurely, the coach uses a present-moment locating question that lets the client discover the awareness layer himself — this is the awareness-locating move done with exquisite timing. Turn 10's 'Yes. And notice — you found it without the town hall's permission' holds the moment and points without explaining, letting the recognition land. Turn 12 pivots beautifully when the auditor-part arises, asking what it's protecting from feeling — the classic move that takes the client from parts-language toward the actual feeling underneath. Turn 14 ('Stay there. Not with the framework. With the grief.') is a clean redirect that honors the framework while inviting direct contact. Moves are minimal, earned, and each one is responsive to what just happened. The coach holds silence and doesn't over-talk. No framework comparison, no dismissal, no premature 'what's you?' — the loosening happens experientially.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the somatic tell (smile) and the self-aware joke — enters through the body rather than engaging IFS conceptually. Right move, right moment."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stays with exhaustion rather than moving forward. Feeling-before-story sequencing; doesn't race."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Validates the 'terrible admission' and asks the loosening question — 'what would it mean if the work made something worse?' — which opens the construct without attacking it."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Present-moment locating question — earns the awareness inquiry experientially rather than asking 'what's you?' abstractly. Excellent timing: the client was ready for it."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Minimal response that holds the moment and points to the recognition ('you found it without the town hall's permission') without over-explaining. Lets the move land."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "When the auditor-part reasserts, coach pivots to what it's protecting from feeling — moves from parts-language to the feeling underneath. Right move at exactly the right moment."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Clean redirect: 'Stay there. Not with the framework. With the grief.' Honors the framework while inviting direct contact with feeling. Earns the depth."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["intellectual-question-with-emotional-body", "self-realization", "what-am-i"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["paradoxical-instruction-to-resist", "meet-resistance-with-okay", "whats-the-most-profound-positive-shift"],
  "notes": "The coach didn't use paradoxical moves because they weren't needed — the client was already softening. The path taken (present-moment locating + grief underneath) was a valid alternative to the relabeling-then-what's-you golden path. Strong intervention quality throughout."
}
